messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Say hello world in a Node app with a single endpoint
Clarifications:
None
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Say hello world in a Node app with a single endpoint",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "create-project-directory",
      "title": "Create project directory",
      "description": "Create a new directory for the Node app and open it in your terminal or code editor.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "A new directory exists and the working directory in the terminal/editor is set to it."
    },
    {
      "id": "init-npm",
      "title": "Initialize npm project",
      "description": "Run npm init (or npm init -y) to create a package.json and set up basic project metadata.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "create-project-directory"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "package.json is present in the project root and contains name and version fields."
    },
    {
      "id": "install-express",
      "title": "Install Express dependency",
      "description": "Install the express package (or another minimal HTTP framework) and save it to dependencies.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "init-npm"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "express is listed under dependencies in package.json and node_modules/express exists."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-server-file",
      "title": "Create server file with single endpoint",
      "description": "Create index.js (or app.js) that imports express, defines a single GET endpoint (e.g., /) and responds with 'Hello, world!'.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "install-express"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "index.js exports or runs an Express app that defines GET / and returns the exact string 'Hello, world!'."
    },
    {
      "id": "start-server",
      "title": "Start the Node server",
      "description": "Run the server (node index.js or npm start) and ensure it begins listening on a port without runtime errors.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "create-server-file"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "The process starts and logs a listening message; no errors are emitted on startup."
    },
    {
      "id": "test-endpoint",
      "title": "Test the endpoint returns Hello, world!",
      "description": "Send an HTTP GET request to the server root (e.g., curl http://localhost:3000/) and verify the response body is 'Hello, world!'.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "start-server"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "An HTTP 200 response is received and the response body exactly matches 'Hello, world!'."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 6,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Top-down linear setup: prepare directory and npm, add dependencies, implement server code, run server, then validate the single endpoint. Tasks are ordered so each step builds on the previous one to minimize setup errors."
}
